orchestrator_notes: Enhancement #44. Protocol says 'NEVER use run_in_background' but background agent execution works excellently in practice. P1 severity, E1 effort. Research: find where the prohibition is, trace if run_in_background actually causes problems, determine if this is just a stale protocol instruction.

sessions.title: Research: run_in_background Protocol Contradiction

original.text: **CRITICAL**: NEVER use `run_in_background=true` - agents must run in foreground for observability.

current_softened_version.text: **DEFAULT**: Run agents in foreground for observability. Use `run_in_background=true` only when the user's project description explicitly requests background execution or parallel monitoring.
current_softened_version.commit_message: fix: Resolve broadcast deadlock + add closeout readiness gate — includes 'Soften run_in_background directive from CRITICAL/NEVER to DEFAULT so LLM can honor explicit user requests for background execution'

prohibition_location.protocol_builder_py: NO references to run_in_background — not involved
prohibition_location.database_templates: NO NEVER/run_in_background text found in SQL backup

findings.what_it_refers_to: Claude Code's Task tool parameter `run_in_background=true`. The prohibition was specifically about spawning subagents via the Task tool with this parameter. It is NOT about a GiljoAI MCP feature. Note: there is a SEPARATE and UNRELATED use of `run_in_background=True` on the Bash tool (for spawning Windows Terminal tabs), which was always RECOMMENDED — see handover 0411 (SUPERSEDED).
findings.stale_explanation: The original 'NEVER' prohibition was written during early January 2026 testing (testrun_Jan_2nd.md) when background execution was untested and observability was a concern. It was already partially fixed in commit 69573dd8 (Feb 22, 2026) which softened it from CRITICAL/NEVER to DEFAULT. However, the current text is STILL overly restrictive — it says 'only when the user's project description explicitly requests background execution', which is unnecessarily conservative given that background execution works reliably.
findings.technical_limitation: NONE FOUND. Searched for: (1) silence_detector — monitors agent health cross-tenant, no dependency on foreground/background execution mode; (2) agent_health_monitor — background monitoring service, independent of agent execution mode; (3) MCP tool calls — work identically in foreground and background Task execution since the MCP server connection is per-API-key session, not per-Task mode; (4) git history — no commits fixing background execution bugs; (5) handovers — no reports of background execution failures. The only observability difference is that foreground agents show real-time output in the orchestrator's terminal, while background agents require polling via TaskOutput. This is a UX preference, not a technical limitation.

task_tool.purpose: Spawn a Claude Code subagent that runs asynchronously, returning a task_id for later polling

bash_tool.purpose: Run a shell command non-blocking (e.g., spawning Windows Terminal tabs)
bash_tool.status: WORKS and was always RECOMMENDED (0411 handover)
bash_tool.current_protocol_text: Not mentioned in current protocol (0411 is SUPERSEDED)

evolution_timeline.reason: Early testing, conservative stance, observability concerns

evolution_timeline.what: NEVER text copied into thin_prompt_generator.py spawning section
evolution_timeline.reason: Protocol codified from test run notes

evolution_timeline.what: Database backup taken — NEVER text NOT in database templates

Recognized as overly restrictive, softened to allow user-requested background execution

evolution_timeline.what: Enhancement #44 filed — protocol still too restrictive
evolution_timeline.reason: Background execution works excellently in practice

proposed_fix.current_text: **DEFAULT**: Run agents in foreground for observability. Use `run_in_background=true` only when the user's project description explicitly requests background execution or parallel monitoring.
proposed_fix.proposed_text: **Spawning Mode**: Use foreground (default) when you need to observe agent output in real-time. Use `run_in_background=true` for independent parallel agents — poll status via `get_workflow_status()`. Background execution is fully supported and reliable.
proposed_fix.rationale: No technical limitation exists. The restriction was a stale early-development precaution. Background execution enables efficient parallel agent orchestration. The guidance should be neutral/encouraging rather than restrictive, letting the orchestrator choose based on the task pattern (sequential vs parallel).
proposed_fix.also_consider: The protocol could explicitly recommend background execution for parallel independent agents (e.g., running a researcher and implementor simultaneously on unrelated files), while recommending foreground for sequential dependent tasks where the orchestrator needs to see output before spawning the next agent.

files_investigated: src/giljo_mcp/services/silence_detector.py (background health monitor, independent of agent execution mode)
src/giljo_mcp/monitoring/agent_health_monitor.py (background monitoring, independent of agent execution mode)
sessions.notes_for_next: Single-line fix in thin_prompt_generator.py:1413. Replace the overly restrictive DEFAULT text with neutral guidance that lets orchestrators freely choose foreground vs background based on task pattern. No other files need changes. The testrun_Jan_2nd.md reference doc is historical and does NOT need updating (it's a completed test log, not active protocol).
sessions.summary: The 'NEVER use run_in_background' prohibition originated in testrun_Jan_2nd.md (Jan 2, 2026) as an early-development precaution when background execution was untested. It was copied into thin_prompt_generator.py and already partially softened in commit 69573dd8 (Feb 22, 2026) from CRITICAL/NEVER to DEFAULT. However, the current text is STILL overly restrictive. No technical limitation exists — silence_detector, health_monitor, MCP connections, and tool calls all work identically in background mode. The fix is a single-line text change in thin_prompt_generator.py:1413 to make the guidance neutral and let orchestrators choose based on task pattern.

sessions.title: Implement: Background Agent Protocol Fix

chain_summary: Chain complete. Research (0801a) confirmed no technical limitation exists for background execution. Implementation (0801b) updated protocol text from restrictive to neutral guidance. Enhancement #44 resolved.
